Styles › Art Nouveau

1890 – 1910 · Europe

Art Nouveau

Nature flowing into line, curve and ornament.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Art Nouveau dissolves the wall into flowing, organic ornament — sinuous 'whiplash' curves, botanical motifs and sculpted ironwork inspired by nature, unifying structure and decoration.

Historical background. A turn-of-the-century reaction against historicism, it embraced new materials (iron, glass) and nature-derived ornament to create a wholly modern decorative language.

Origin. Brussels, Paris, Barcelona, Vienna, c.1890–1910.

Timeline

1893

Horta's Hôtel Tassel

1900

Guimard's Paris Métro entrances

1906

Gaudí's Casa Batlló

1910

Casa Milà completed

Key Architects

Victor HortaHector GuimardAntoni GaudíOtto Wagner

Influential Projects

Casa Batlló

Antoni Gaudí · 1906
Barcelona, Spain

Casa Milà (La Pedrera)

Antoni Gaudí · 1910
Barcelona, Spain

Hôtel Tassel

Victor Horta · 1893
Brussels, Belgium

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Nature as ornamentSinuous 'whiplash' lineUnity of art & structureNew materials (iron, glass)Total design

The Thinking

Line itself becomes alive — curving, growing, botanical — as iron, stone and glass are sculpted into a unified organic ornament that erases the line between structure and decoration.

Values It Represents

BeautyNatureCraftModernity

Why It Emerged

A reaction against historical revival and industrial ugliness — a fresh, nature-based modern decorative art for a new century.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingSculptural masses with undulating, plant-like surfaces and soft edges.
Form LanguageFlowing, asymmetric, organic forms; curving walls and balconies.
CompositionDynamic, asymmetric, growing compositions.
ProportionOrganic and varied; ornament-led.
GeometryCurves, parabolas, sinuous lines and botanical forms.
RhythmFlowing, irregular, plant-like rhythm.
SymmetryAsymmetrical and naturalistic.
ScaleIntimate to ornate-monumental.
Roof TypesCurving, undulating roofs; sculpted parapets and chimneys.
Window ProportionIrregular organic openings with curving stone and stained-glass.
EntranceSculptural, flowing, ornate portal.
Façade ArticulationCurving stone, sculpted ironwork, ceramic and stained-glass in flowing botanical ornament.

04 — Materials

Materials

Wrought iron

Sculptural metalwork for balconies, grilles and ornament.

Carved stone

Solid, durable and rooted in place — a defining exterior material of the style.

Stained glass

Admits light and dissolves the wall between inside and out.

Ceramic tile (trencadís)

Colourful glazed surface for decorative façade pattern.

Stucco

Smooth render that unifies the wall and catches light.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Soft, organic and jewel-toned — muted naturals lit by stained-glass colour.

Primary

Soft Stone
#dccfb4
Sage
#9aa882

Accent

Peacock Teal
#2f7a78
Lilac
#9a7aa8
Gold
#c8a14a

Material Colors

Iron Black
#3a3631
Stained Glass
#5a8a7a

Typical Combinations

  • +Stone + iron + stained glass
  • +Sage + peacock teal + gold

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionIrregular organic openings with curving stone mullions and stained glass.
EmphasisFlowing — vertical growth softened by sinuous curves.
Shading DevicesDeep organic reveals, balconies and overhangs.
BalconiesSculptural wrought-iron balconies with botanical motifs.
FramesCurving stone and sinuous iron frames.
OpeningsOrganic, varied, plant-like openings.
Architectural DetailsWhiplash ironwork, carved foliage, ceramic mosaic, stained glass.
OrnamentationCentral and celebrated — flowing nature-derived ornament unifies the whole façade.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Stained glass and organic openings filter coloured, dappled daylight.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Warm, sculptural; iron and glass catch the light.

Night Façade Appearance

Glowing stained glass and lit ironwork; warm and jewel-like.

Recommendations

Warm 2700K; backlight stained glass; graze sculptural ironwork.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

Townhouses & mansionsApartment buildingsDepartment storesCafés & restaurantsPavilionsMetro/transport entrances

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Lead with flowing organic ornament
  • Use sinuous whiplash lines
  • Sculpt wrought-iron balconies and grilles
  • Add stained glass and ceramic mosaic
  • Curve walls, openings and roofline
  • Unify structure and decoration
  • Draw motifs from plants and nature
  • Use soft naturals with jewel accents

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Rigid orthogonal geometry
  • ✕Sparse, applied ornament with no flow
  • ✕Historical-revival motifs
  • ✕Straight, mechanical lines everywhere
  • ✕Cheap finishes betraying the craft

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Art Nouveau townhouse exterior, sinuous curving stone façade, organic wrought-iron balconies, botanical ornament, stained glass, undulating roofline, warm light, architectural photography --ar 2:3 --style raw

Façade Concept

Art Nouveau façade, whiplash ironwork, carved foliage, curving stone mullions, stained-glass openings, organic ornament, frontal elevation, high detail

Night View

Art Nouveau building exterior at night, glowing stained glass, lit sculptural ironwork, warm jewel tones, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of an Art Nouveau building, undulating sculpted roofscape, organic parapets and chimneys, drone view, warm light

Street Perspective

Street view of an Art Nouveau façade, flowing ironwork balconies, organic stone portal, stained glass, café below, documentary photography

Masterplan

Art Nouveau quarter masterplan, sinuous street wall of organic façades, planted boulevard, axonometric, soft natural palette

Related Styles

Art Deco

Glamorous, geometric, decorative — strong lines and symmetry.

France → USA → GlobalGlamorous
1837 – 1901

Victorian

Exuberant eclectic ornament and picturesque silhouettes.

Britain → GlobalEclectic
1850 – 1920

Beaux-Arts

Monumental classical grandeur, richly composed.
